payload.summary: All four Q-responses implemented. Session closing with deferred items noted.

implementations.action: Added 3 non-ICESCR discriminator worked examples to /for/educators: climate policy (emissions plateau), history (Roman Republic fall), media literacy (institutional trust decline). Each maps to the 5-dimension scoring rubric without ICESCR content.

Banner now shows on first page load per session only, then hides via sessionStorage. Reappears on new sessions. Reduces cumulative persuasion knowledge activation while maintaining FTC-defensible disclosure.

Varied 12 of 32 prohibition instances across talking-points.mdx (5), why-act.mdx (5), and index.astro (2). Syntactic structures now include: 'Facing X should concern every voter', 'Receiving X should not be acceptable', 'X is precisely the outcome binding rights exist to prevent', 'Workers deserve Y when Z'. Loss-frame semantics preserved throughout. Covenant article instances (20) retained — the declarative 'No X should' form works well as article-specific rights statements.

Added 'Strengthening Your Letter' section to template-letters.mdx with three subsections: (1) Committee jurisdiction guidance for SFRC, HELP, Judiciary, Finance with sample language activating role identity; (2) Authority signals — ABA treaty recommendation, Carter Center, USCRC, HRW, Amnesty; (3) Domestic polling — Gallup healthcare (60-70%), Pew education, BLS job transition, framing domestic rights support as existing consensus missing legal framework.

deferred: A/B testing prohibition vs positive rights framing (requires analytics infrastructure beyond current CF Web Analytics)
Researcher page AI-collaboration methodology note (low priority — researcher page already leads with limitations)
Nature 2025 counterattitudinal leverage (monitoring — may integrate if further research confirms finding)
Cross-lens closing variety ('Human Rights; Nothing More, Nothing Less' on researcher/developer pages)
payload.session_status: closing — all accepted recommendations implemented or explicitly deferred with reasoning

claims.text: All four Q-response implementations complete in commit 116d29a.
ABA has recommended U.S. ratification of major human rights treaties.
claims.confidence_basis: ABA advocacy page references treaty ratification priorities; specific ICESCR mention needs verification against current ABA policy resolutions.

epistemic_flags: ABA endorsement claim (c2) references their general treaty ratification advocacy — specific current ICESCR resolution status unverified
Domestic polling figures cited are for general economic rights concepts, not ICESCR-specific support — this is noted in the text but represents an inference bridge
